tasks.direction_relation_note: First executable slice for pre-launch planning visibility and shared validation.
tasks.title: Add external `akra doctor` for planning health before shell startup
tasks.description: Expose a non-interactive workspace-root command that reports planning state, queue summary, proposal summary, and the first blocking validation issue by reusing shared planning validation logic rather than inventing a CLI-only checker.
tasks.priority_reason: The operator explicitly needs pre-launch planning visibility to trust long-running self-host automation.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Direct execution slice for Phase 1 state-language simplification.
tasks.title: Unify operator-facing shell state language around current state, cause, and next action
tasks.description: Audit operator-facing status and pause copy across shell presentation, automation overlay, queue overlay, and planning recovery surfaces, then implement a canonical vocabulary and projection path without changing automation policy semantics in the same slice.
tasks.priority_reason: Still the main Phase 1 UX follow-up once workspace lifecycle visibility exists.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Bootstraps the same default planning scaffold from the normal shell prompt.
tasks.title: Add external `akra init` for default planning scaffold creation
tasks.description: Expose a workspace-root bootstrap command that creates the default planning scaffold and refuses to overwrite existing active planning files, while reusing the shared planning bootstrap service instead of maintaining a second scaffold path.
tasks.priority_reason: The product should let operators bootstrap planning before opening the TUI, but this follows shared doctor visibility.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Defines safe reset targets and destructive-scope rules for long-lived planning workspaces.
tasks.title: Add shared reset semantics for `akra reset` and `:reset`
tasks.description: Define queue, directions, and full-workspace reset behavior behind shared planning services so reset paths clearly state rewritten artifacts, refuse unsafe directions-only resets with live tasks, and support both external and in-shell entrypoints without diverging rules.
tasks.priority_reason: Reset becomes important once doctor and init exist, but it must land with explicit safety semantics.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps `:doctor`, `:init`, and `:reset` aligned with the same external lifecycle model.
tasks.title: Wire `:doctor`, `:init`, and `:reset` to the shared planning lifecycle services
tasks.description: Add in-shell entrypoints that expose the same planning doctor, init, and reset semantics with richer overlays, staged review, or confirmation where needed, without creating a second planning lifecycle model inside the TUI.
tasks.priority_reason: Internal command parity matters, but shared services and external operators come first.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Turns queue inspection into the operator work board described in the blueprint.
tasks.title: Reframe queue inspection as now, next, candidates, and blocked work
tasks.description: Change queue-facing projections so the operator can distinguish the current executable task, near-future queued work, promotable proposals, and blocked or skipped items without reading planner internals.
tasks.priority_reason: High-leverage follow-up once shell vocabulary is stable.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Makes queue-driven continuation feel predictable instead of mysterious.
tasks.title: Make automation pause reasons point to one recovery action
tasks.description: Restructure automation status and controls so every pause category clearly states what paused, why it paused, and which surface or action resumes progress.
tasks.priority_reason: Directly supports explainable automation, the main differentiator.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Reduces the setup cost of the first successful planning loop.
tasks.title: Simplify first-run planning review around the simple-mode happy path
tasks.description: Refocus planning onboarding, staged review copy, and queue-idle explanation so a new workspace reaches its first successful queue-driven continuation with minimal setup friction.
tasks.priority_reason: Phase 2 depends on making the first planning loop easier to understand.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Connects resumed conversations to the current work-management state.
tasks.title: Surface planning and queue context immediately after session resume
tasks.description: Ensure resumed sessions show whether planning is active, what the current queue summary is, and whether automation can continue without forcing the operator to open multiple overlays.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Architecture follow-up that unlocks safer UX iteration for Phase 1 and Phase 2.
tasks.title: Extract a status and projection layer from shell presentation hotspots
tasks.description: Separate operator wording and status projection from layout and runtime plumbing so future UX changes can land in smaller, safer slices.
tasks.priority_reason: Needed to keep follow-up UX changes reviewable and decoupled from layout churn.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Preserves release confidence while operator surfaces evolve.
tasks.title: Codify terminal validation expectations for Phase 1 operator-surface work
tasks.description: Update validation and operator runbook flows so shell, queue, and planning UX changes always record the real-terminal checks that were actually run.
